title: Define proof envelope MIME type and serialization format

artifacts.description: Narrative spec: rationale for application/bolyra-proof+cbor, IANA registration template, content negotiation rules, COSE_Sign1 wrapper structure, and version/discriminator field semantics
CBOR CDDL schema defining ProofEnvelope, HumanProofPayload, AgentProofPayload, DelegationChain, ProofSystem enum (groth16=0, plonk=1), PublicSignalsLayout tag, and version uint
ABNF grammar for the Content-Type header value including parameter rules (version, proof-system) per RFC 6838 structured syntax suffix conventions
TypeScript module exporting serializeProofEnvelope() and deserializeProofEnvelope() using cbor-x; encodes version, proof-system discriminator, public-signals layout tag, and raw proof bytes
TypeScript types: ProofEnvelope, ProofSystem enum, PublicSignalsLayout, EnvelopeVersion constant, and HumanProofEnvelope / AgentProofEnvelope / DelegationEnvelope branded subtypes

Round-trip conformance tests: serialize then deserialize for each proof type (human, agent, delegation) and each proof system (groth16, plonk); assert byte-for-byte determinism and schema validity against CDDL

Static test vectors: hex-encoded canonical CBOR bytes for version=1 human-groth16, agent-plonk, and delegation-groth16 envelopes with known public signals; used by conformance runners in other languages
Developer guide: how to attach application/bolyra-proof+cbor in HTTP and MCP transports, content negotiation fallback to JSON, migration path from existing raw JSON arrays, and SDK usage examples

steps: Audit existing SDK proof output: read sdk/src/prove.ts and sdk/src/types.ts to catalog current BigInt-string JSON array shapes for human, agent, and delegation proofs

Design CBOR CDDL schema: define ProofEnvelope top-level map with mandatory fields (version: 1, proof-system: 0|1, layout-tag: tstr, proof: bstr, public-signals: [bstr]), write spec/proof-envelope.cddl
Write ABNF grammar for Content-Type parameter syntax and add IANA registration template section to spec/proof-envelope-media-type.md
Add cbor-x as a direct dependency in sdk/package.json (already present in snarkjs transitive tree; pin to ^1.5.0 for deterministic map encoding)
Implement envelope.ts: serializeProofEnvelope() maps proof-system enum and public-signals BigInt array to CBOR bstr array; deserializeProofEnvelope() reverses and validates required keys
Export new types and functions from sdk/src/index.ts without breaking existing verifyHandshake / proveHandshake public API signatures
Write conformance round-trip tests in spec/conformance/envelope-roundtrip.test.ts covering all three proof types × both proof systems; assert deterministic encoding (same input → same bytes)
Generate and commit static test vectors in spec/conformance/envelope-vectors.json using the serializer against fixture proofs from existing fast-circuit test outputs
Validate generated CBOR against the CDDL schema in CI using cddl-validator npm package as a dev dependency
Update docs/proof-envelope-guide.md with HTTP Accept/Content-Type negotiation examples, MCP tool metadata pattern, and a migration note for existing consumers of raw JSON arrays
Add envelope-roundtrip test suite to npm run test in root package.json alongside existing circuit and contract test suites
Run full test suite (npm test) and typecheck (cd sdk && npm run typecheck) to confirm no regressions, then sign and commit with DCO
dependencies: cddl-validator (dev dependency for CI schema validation)
existing sdk/src/prove.ts proof output shapes (must not break)
existing verifyHandshake / proveHandshake public API (additive change only)
snarkjs BigInt public-signals array format (determines CBOR bstr encoding strategy)
RFC 6838 structured syntax suffix rules for +cbor media type registration
COSE_Sign1 structure (informational reference for envelope design, not a hard runtime dependency in Phase 1)
